concerns.text: Task T017 implements 'retrospective power analysis' (observed power), which semantically violates FR-007. FR-007 explicitly mandates an 'a priori power analysis' to estimate minimum sample size *before* analysis to validate the study design. Retrospective power is tautological and does not satisfy the requirement to validate the design prior to execution.

Task T038 instructs fetching a 'verified proxy' or falling back to the Numenta Anomaly Benchmark (NAB) dataset as a structural placeholder. This violates Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) and Principle III (Data Hygiene) by introducing an irrelevant, non-canonical dataset that cannot reproduce the specific 'Political IAT' study results. The spec/plan requires halting with a clear error if no real source is found, not substituting unrelated data.

Task T022 describes alpha sweep logic as re-evaluating significance at thresholds 'including standard and relaxed levels' without explicitly listing the mandated set {0.01, 0.05, 0.10} from FR-004. This creates a risk of implementation drift where the specific required thresholds are not tested, violating the deterministic requirement of FR-004.

Task T024b schedules the fitting of the secondary binary ideology model *after* the robustness checks (T021-T023). FR-006 and US-2 require the binary split to be part of the robustness/sensitivity checks. The current ordering risks breaking the dependency chain where binary model results are needed to fully populate the robustness metrics CSV (T026) before reporting, potentially omitting a required deliverable.
